completed_tasks.name: P2 Task 1 - Accessibility runtime status slice

remaining_tasks.name: P2 Task 2 - First semantic UI capability slice

human_actions_pending.action: If Ghosthand is reinstalled before verification, re-enable Ghosthand Accessibility in Android accessibility settings or append the service back into enabled_accessibility_services.
human_actions_pending.context: This device drops Ghosthand from the enabled accessibility-services list after reinstall, which makes /state.accessibility fall back to disabled until the service is re-enabled.

decisions.decision: Bound P2 Task 1 to accessibility status only.
decisions.rationale: The phase needed the AccessibilityService capability boundary and observability before any tree or action endpoints were added.

decisions.decision: Expose accessibility state using implemented/enabled/healthy/status rather than guessing action readiness.
decisions.rationale: This keeps /state honest while still making the service observable from the app and local API.

next_action: Start with P2 Task 2 and keep scope tight: add the first minimal semantic UI capability without introducing root or recovery logic.
context_notes: P1 is in good shape: /health, /state, /device, and /foreground all work on 127.0.0.1:5583. P2 Task 1 is complete and verified on-device. GhostAccessibilityService is registered, can be enabled in settings, and /state.accessibility reports real status. The home screen shows accessibility status too. The biggest resume nuance is device-state verification: reinstalling the APK can remove Ghosthand from the device's enabled accessibility-services list, so the connected state may need to be restored manually before verification.
